description: The double-undo chain ends at x, d/dx ln(x) = 1/x holds numerically, and ln(ab) = ln(a)+ln(b) survives independent (a,b) samples
\ln as a command (upright), the whole \ln(x) braced inside the exponent of e, \left( \right) around tall contents
The page presents ln as the inverse of e^x (the chain ending at plain x) and the log law as the mirror of the exponent rule
Text and math read well; markers kept
